observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Create a separate Inventory Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE
required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and approve the Physical
Inventory Plan necessary for the conduct and complete physical inventory;
b. Designate or appoint a General Services Officer (GSO) who will be in
charge in the management and monitoring of municipal properties; and
c. Instruct the duly created Inventory Team, the designated GSO and the
Accounting Office to strictly follow the general guidelines, procedural
guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and
accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs,
procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without
available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and
required disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under
COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of inventory taking for one-time
cleansing of PPE.
1.2. We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC Municipal
Accountant to make detailed evaluation and tracing of the noted unaccounted
beginning balances to reflect the reliable balances of these accounts in the
Financial Statements.
1.3. We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the prescribed property
ledger cards for Local Road Networks, disclose the information for the said
property in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the above
cited regulations and provide separate depreciation for the road and the
road components;
c. Municipal Treasurer to periodically maintain a complete Report on Local
Road Networks, prepare the Road Map and keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Cards for all roads and their components and furnish
copies thereof the concerned offices, particularly the City Accountant, as
basis for reconciliation with the accounting records;
d. Inventory Committee, in coordination with the City Engineering Office, (i)
conduct physical inventory of all existing local road networks in the City
and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Local Road
Network to be reconciled with the accounting records; and (ii) furnish a
copy of such report to the Audit Team for verification and audit.
1.4. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a Committee on Disposal to
initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of those properties in accordance with
afore-cited provisions in order not to expose those properties to elements that
cause further deterioration or loss of salvaged value and to generate additional
income thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-
end in time for the closing of the books and submit to the Municipal
Accountant; and
b. Municipal Accountant to revert back to the Cash in Bank and proper
liability accounts the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks
at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements
under the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring
of the balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
inform the concerned suppliers for the warranty deposit and require them to prepare a
letter request regarding the return/refund of the amount due them of which claim shall
be supported with the photocopy of the disbursement voucher showing such amount
was deducted. In case, the concerned suppliers were not anymore existing or no
response was received from them, effect the necessary adjustment in the LGU’s
financial statement by debiting the account Guaranty Deposit Payable and crediting
the account Government Equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant
to prepare and maintain a complete schedule of liabilities of the Municipality
indicating all pertinent and relevant information to support the balances reflected
in the ledgers and to enable to properly monitor funds which are due for return,
liquidation or payment and reversion to the Municipality’s funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and OIC-Municipal Accountant cause
the submission of the documents required by items by Items 9.1 and 9.1.1
of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 for evaluation as basis
for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action;
b. Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents
prescribed under the circular to ensure compliance with RIRR of RA 9184
before processing payments in order to establish the legality, validity and
propriety of the disbursement;
c. BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
9184 to establish the regularity of the procurement process and award of
contract; and
d. Municipal officials concerned to ensure that the projects undertaken by the
Municipality are implemented and completed on time so that the intended
beneficiaries could enjoy the benefits at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
pay only upon delivery of goods and services for all procurement undertaken by the
Municipality.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to strictly
observe the provision of Republic Act (RA) 9184 for the procurement of goods
before entering into a contract with suppliers.

a. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
submit justification why the insurance was not renewed for the Calatagan Public
Market and to regularly monitor the renewal of insurance of all government
property in accordance with RA 656 in order to protect the interest of the
municipal government.

b. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC Municipal Accountant to
effect the necessary adjustments by reclassifying the unexpired portion of the
insurance premiums of the insured municipal properties and to strictly observe the
matching principle in the preparation of financial statements in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly to the Chart of Accounts in the
recording of LGU share from PCSO Charity Fund in accordance with the
aforementioned regulations ; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that Share from PCSO be treated as income
and can be appropriated, allotted and obligated only for duly approved programs,
projects and activities during the budget year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that transfer of unspent DRRMF to Trust Fund be
supported with actual cash to preclude misstatements of the Cash in Bank and the
receivables/payables accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accounting Office to
prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liability–DRRM account per
year of transfer as well as for those funds received from other agencies.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant
to ensure that DRRM funds received from various government agencies and from
other sources be treated as special trust liability recorded under Trust Liabilities –
DRRMF in the Trust Fund books. Prepare the necessary accounting entry to
recognize the adjustment/transfer of the said balances to account Trust Liabilities
– DRRMF.

We recommended the Management for the implementation of most of its
development projects and programs under the 20% Development Fund for CY 2021
and to strive more for the 100 percent completion of planned projects and programs in
the future for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all department heads and
the Bid and Award Committee to adhere strictly with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2009-001 on the prompt submission of the copies of perfected
Contracts/Purchase Orders and their supporting documents within five (5)
working days after execution/ issuance thereof, for timely review/evaluation of
the legal and/or technical aspects of the contracts and for management to be
informed early of any deficiencies noted in audit.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the concerned municipal
official who is in-charge in the inspection and acceptance of purchased goods
and infrastructure projects to submit to COA the letter notifying the Audit
Team on every delivery for post-audit reference regardless of the modes of
procurement used.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned municipal employees to submit the documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to consider that those claims are allowable in audit. These parcels of land were already titled and fully accounted in the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer commented that the remarks will be considered in the ensuing year. The Municipal Assessor to include these parcels in the appropriations the titling of land owned by the Municipality.
observations.reason: This matter was not given attention due to COVID 19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to clarify delays, collect liquidated damages if warranted, and submit all procurement documentation.
observations.managementAction: No action done. The Municipal Management did not provide the requested documents for evaluation; no appropriate action taken.
observations.reason: No action taken; documentation not made available to auditors.

observations.recommendation: Prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake disposal to prevent further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: No action done by Management. The MDRRMO noted the recommendation, but disposal not yet undertaken.
observations.reason: Management did not prioritize; lack of action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Create/reconstitute an Inventory Committee for the conduct of complete and
detailed physical count of all the Municipality’s properties taking into account
the provisions mandated under COA Circular 2020-006, particularly on the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts;
b. Instruct the Inventory Committee to properly utilize the required inventory
reports stated on the above-mentioned regulations;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to:
i. work coordinately with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer on the
inventory of Infrastructure Assets such as Land Improvements, Local Road
Network, Flood Control System, Water Supply System and Other Public
Infrastructures.
ii. Exert efforts on all causes of discrepancies towards the reconciliation of
their respective records to ascertain the accuracy of the PPE balances in
the financial statements;
d. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary accounting entries
to reclassify the Land costing ₱2,486,602.00 and book up the donated
ambulance valued at ₱1,585,063.00.
e. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer for the enactment of the Sangguniang
Bayan to appropriate funds in ensuing year for the filling up of the General
Services Officer to have a permanent official who will assume the duties and
responsibilities in the office and to address the perennial issues relative to the
supply and property management of the Municipality.

We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare the breakdown of the total delinquent taxes
as to the principal amount, interest and penalty to come with a comparable
data with that of the Municipal Accountant’s record;
b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of
RPT and SET Receivables and the total delinquency taxes and effect the
necessary adjustments, if any;
c) Municipal Accountant to adopt the new guidelines in recording the total
amount collectible at the beginning of the year; and
d) Municipal Treasurer to monitor payment of real property tax due for the
current year and exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Local School Board:

i. Identify priority areas listed in the School Improvement Plan (SIP) in order
to come up with a reliable SEF Budget to ensure effective delivery of basic
education services;
ii. Submit justification why funds are realigned from one item/class of
expenditure in spite provision of Section 336 of RA 7160;
iii. Refrain from approving ordinance on the realignment of funds which are
not same item/class of expenditures in consonance with; and
iv. Enforce the strict compliance of the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1,
series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and
Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government in the
preparation of SEF Budget.

We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant
prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the erroneous entry of the
total shares of LGUs in the proceed derived from Energy Resources to Share from
National Wealth in the General Fund instead of Due to LGUs in the Trust Fund to
present the correct year-end balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to -
• immediately submit the lacking documents to substantiate
aforementioned BGCM transactions and ensure that all documentary
requirements for emergency purchase are complied with before
certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the
disbursement vouchers;
• prepare and submit the subsidiary ledger for the SAGF-BGCM in
compliance with the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs
for fair presentation, transparency and accountability of the funds
utilized;
• use the appropriate accounts in recording transactions in compliance
with COA Circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31, 2020 prescribing the
Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units; and
b. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all procurements
implemented by the Municipality are in accordance with the Government
Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) issuances.

For future similar financial assistance, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Disbursing Officer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare
Development Officer and the barangay officials to assist fully in the compilation of
the documentary requirements in order to efficiently facilitate the immediate
liquidation of the cash advance in consonance with the provision of DSWD MC
No. 9, Series of 2020.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Disbursing
Officer and the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer to submit written
justification on the delay in the refund of P156,000.00 to DSWD Field Office No. IV-
A.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) to:

a. Fully utilize the fund received for calamity related expenses to ensure that the
targeted beneficiaries will receive the necessary assistance from the
government. For the unexpended balance of the grant, he should
coordinate with the Local Finance committee for the possible use of the fund
for other PPAs which are authorized in the Memorandum of Agreement;
b. Comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2014-002 and utilized the
funds in accordance with the specific intent of the donors so that the funds
will cater the immediate needs of those affected by the Taal Volcano
eruption; and
c. Submit explanation/justification on the charging of gasoline expenses
which is not related with the intended purpose of the fund received.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
review liquidation reports of the cash advances pertaining to payment of hazard
pay and require those concerned personnel to immediately submit the required
documents such as authority from the head of agency/office and accomplishment
reports. Likewise, submit written justification on the claims of Hazard pay during
the entire period of ECQ and certification from the Head of Agency stating that
SRA claimants were physically report for work on said period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRMMO) to
strictly comply with NDRRMC, DBM and DIG Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 in the allocation of the Quick
Response Fund (QRF) and Mitigation Fund (MF) on the preparation of
LDRRM Investment Plan;
b. Bids and Award Committee to submit a written explanation why
procurement of welfare goods was done on piecemeal basis and for the
non-submission of resolution recommending award of contract to a
supplier who are legally, technically, and financially capable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:

a. Fully utilize the programs/projects/activities embodied in the GAD Plan and
Budget in compliance with PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-
01; and
b. Innovate ways for the implementation of programs under the new normal
and make use of the virtual/social platforms to be able to optimally achieve
the goal of a GAD-responsive governance and environment even at times
of quarantine brought by the pandemic.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure the sufficiency of the
employee’s net pay after deducting the loan amortizations prior to
approving loans;
b. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and loan
amortizations due to avoid incurrence of penalties and remit the same
immediately to GSIS; and
c. AAO and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSIS for the
reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the unpaid
premiums of PS and GS with a total amount of P223,170.28.

observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer agreed that it is really proper to maintain periodic physical count of PPE in order to have a balance inventory and regular submission of RCPPE be taken diligently in order to have a well-taken recorded PPE and other capital outlays funded by the agency. Nonetheless, all recommendations are well taken and necessary adjustments will be taken in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: This matter was not prioritized by Management due to COVID 19 pandemic.

observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer commented that the recommendations will be followed in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to:
• Undertake careful projection of revenues for the ensuing year and conduct proper review of past collection performances. Also, regular reconciliation be made by the Municipal Treasurer and OIC, Municipal Accountant to avoid unreconciled balances. And if the cause of discrepancy is justifiable, disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements is needed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Local Finance Committee was required to prepare the proposed measures in order to have a realistic projection of revenues for the year and conduct periodic assessment. For CY 2020, the targeted collection was not met. However, the Municipal Management required concerned municipal officials to undertake measures in order to have a realistic projection of revenue.
observations.reason: Due to COVID 19 pandemic, the targeted collections were not attained.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the various liquidation reports if not yet submitted; and
• Municipal Accountant and concerned officials to review the composition of the accounts under the Due to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous recording/inclusion in the Due to NGAs account to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments.
• Municipal Health Officer to enforce the utilization and implementation of fund received from DOH for the health facility enhancement of P1.3 Million. Likewise, see to it that respective time frame and schedule are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delay in its implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed that the submitted documents are still for further evaluation by Management. As to the balance of fund transfers of completed projects and counterpart of Municipality, identify the amounts to be remitted to the source agencies or the amounts to be reverted in the General Fund for possible programming/appropriation.
observations.reason: No action taken by Management. This matter was not fully acted upon and documents still for evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Government Accounting Office the List of Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of the books to the reversion back to cash in bank and payable accounts of the amount of unreleased checks in order to fairly present the cash in bank and other affected accounts in the financial statements;
• For the checks which have become stale, the Municipal Treasurer to send written notice to the payees for the unclaimed existence of checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of new checks; and
• Municipal Accountant to effect the bank necessary adjustments of reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer informed the Management. Regular preparation of reconciliation statements has been done by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• compel the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly observe the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184 in the awarding of contract to legitimate contractor; and
• the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents as enumerated above by the Audit Team and COA-TAS for the final inspection and technical evaluation of the said projects and contracts.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman appreciated the Audit Team’s recommendations and assured that the BAC is complying with the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184. The Municipal Engineer submitted the documents and the final technical evaluation of the projects and contracts are for evaluation.
observations.reason: There were cases that municipal employees are already retired or not connected in the municipality; documents still for further evaluation by Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and other concerned municipal employees to submit the lacking documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to consider that those claims are allowable in audit.
observations.managementAction: The submitted documents were made. These parcels of land were already titled in the name of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council/MPDC and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to determine the viability of the projects to be funded out of 20% Development Fund before the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to ensure its implementation and that those programs/projects completed within the intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Development Council/MPDC implemented most projects under the 20% Development Fund. The MPDC to coordinate and ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended for development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO to enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the Municipality under 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs completed within the intended time to avoid delay. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the utilization of 5% LDRRMF and ensure programs and projects are aligned with their intended appropriation.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO enforced the implementation of the 70% Mitigation Fund and projects are being implemented. The Municipal Budget Officer monitored the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Occurrence of Taal Volcano and COVID 19 pandemic affected some implementations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:
• Fully utilize the programs/projects/activities embodied in the GAD Plan and Budget in compliance with PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01; and
• Innovate ways for the implementation of programs under the new normal and make use of the virtual/social platforms to be able to optimally achieve the goal of a GAD-responsive governance and environment even at times of quarantine brought by the pandemic.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person admitted that the implementation of some projects, programs and activities stipulated in the 2020 GAD Plan and Budget was affected due to global pandemic and will adopt innovative means.
observations.reason: Limitations imposed by the outbreak of COVID-19 pandemic in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the real property tax share to all the barangays within the prescribed period to augment the limited financial resources and to allow the immediate use of the fund for regular expenses and priority projects in compliance with Article 362 (b) of the IRR of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was informed to remit the barangay shares regularly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer be compelled to submit the required documents and that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the contractor for the refund of the cost deficiency noted by the COA TAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted the documents. The cost deficiency of ₱43,648.80 was sustained by the COA TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Accountant to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assist users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information relevant to decision-making.
observations.managementAction: The ICO-Municipal Accountant adopted the proper disclosure in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in electronic format within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman replied that the BAC is complying with the provisions and the procurement monitoring report was prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant to:
• reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG to identify the unremitted balances and exhaust all available means to secure documents and journals to trace back those old balances and identify the specific tax payers. Otherwise, if those agencies confirm no outstanding claims, request for the reversion of such payables following applicable guidelines; and
• review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and ensure that these taxes are remitted on the prescribed period to avoid penalties and surcharges.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) commented that they are aware of the obligation to remit on time the correct premiums and are reconciling with GSIS discrepancies and some names included were already transferred to other government agency.
observations.reason: There were cases that balances were old and no longer supported with documents; coordination with GSIS ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and OIC Municipal Accountant to conduct complete physical count and reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records; secure necessary documents to support PPEs; review composition of accounts and identify appropriate classification; and maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards.
observations.managementAction: No action done by Management. The LGU has no enough manpower to conduct this activity since the position of GSO is not yet created due to budget constraint.
observations.reason: Unavailability of documents and no enough manpower; GSO position not created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and submit documents on the composition of RPT/SET receivables and present reliable RPT/SET receivables in the financial statements; conduct verification/legal measures for collection; and submit copy of the legal basis for the .5% assessment rate of Solar Philippines Calatagan Corporation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconciled records with concerned offices. There was increase of delinquent taxes from last year. The Municipal Treasurer submitted the basis of 5% assessment rate for Solar Philippines.
observations.reason: The RPT/SET receivables in financial statements still unreconciled; COVID-19 hampered collection efforts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with concerned heads to obtain information on those long outstanding accounts; verify reported balances; review composition of the accounts under Due to LGUs and identify appropriate classification for the items identified; and secure documentation before effecting adjusting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant to exhaust efforts to fully ascertain the balances, and to reconcile with subsidiary ledgers. Records still being gathered to determine the validity and legality of balances.
observations.reason: Some balances not yet identified; records being gathered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the Municipal Accountant take up as Accounts Payable. Also, adjustment entries should be made to fairly present the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Cancellation of unreleased/stale checks were done and Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) were prepared to take up accounts payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review composition of the accounts under Due to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified; secure proper documentation before effecting adjusting entries; and review fund transfers and liquidation reports and remit unexpended balances to source agencies or revert back to General Fund as necessary.
observations.managementAction: There are balances that were not yet identified. The Municipal Accountant to immediately remit balances to source agencies if confirmed; liquidation reports were submitted for some projects and balances to be remitted in CY 2021.
observations.reason: Records were not available; some fund balances to be remitted in subsequent year.

observations.managementAction: Regular reconciliation of bank statements has been done by the Accounting Office.
The Municipal heads to enforce utilization of funds and submit liquidation reports; projects sourced from national agencies to be implemented; if unutilized amounts to be reverted.
observations.reason: Some projects for which funds were received had unutilized balances; liquidation to be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with RA 9184 and that the Municipal Mayor compel the concerned to submit the necessary documentation, clarify inconsistencies, and strictly comply with procurement rules.
observations.managementAction: Compliance with procurement rules strengthened. Documents submitted to the Audit Team for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund, that projects be viable before inclusion in AIP and monitor implementation.
observations.managementAction: Most projects under 20% DF have been implemented. The Municipal Development Council to ensure viability and proper planning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended issuance of appropriate policy directives; ensure GAD database is operational and GAD Budget is prepared in the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality took steps to implement GAD measures and to adhere to PCW and DILG guidance.
observations.reason: Low utilization of GAD PPAs and COVID-19 affected implementation.

observations.recommendation: Charge the monetization to appropriate appropriations and ensure compliance with budget ordinance and DBM Budget Circular No. 2002-1 dated January 14, 2002.
observations.managementAction: Monetization followed appropriation ordinance and payments were processed in accordance with provisions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended reconciliation with BIR, GSIS and PAG-IBIG to determine correctness of unpaid premiums and remit balances if due.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation with the concerned agencies was being undertaken; some names were already transferred to other agencies.
observations.reason: Some names no longer with municipality; coordination with GSIS ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Discourage practices that undermine planning and budgeting; ensure integration of planning and budgeting at local level.
observations.managementAction: The municipality revised procedures and is more careful in preparing Annual Budget aligned with AIP/APP/LDP.
observations.reason: Preparation improved but some practices persisted.

observations.recommendation: Evaluate the cost/benefit of maintaining time deposit and explore options for better cash management consistent with PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Office evaluated its cash management and reduced idle balances; deposits reviewed.

observations.recommendation: Conduct thorough study and evaluation on actual workloads and manpower requirements before engaging job order workers.
observations.managementAction: A study and evaluation on the actual manpower requirements was undertaken and the number of job orders minimized.

observations.recommendation: Expedite implementation of not-yet started and ongoing projects and organize the composition of the Board of Directors of Calatagan Water District to make completed projects usable and operational.
observations.managementAction: Some of the completed projects were implemented; efforts made to operationalize idle projects.
observations.reason: Some projects still idle; further action required.

observations.recommendation: Implement rules for collections, impose penalties for defaulting lessees, and create ordinances to promote the market and enforce collections.
observations.managementAction: Enforcement of collections improved; some collections made; ordinance yet to be enacted.
observations.reason: COVID-19 pandemic hindered full enforcement; ordinance not yet enacted.

observations.recommendation: Require refund of disallowed amounts and refrain from incurring additional Personal Services beyond limitations.
observations.managementAction: The municipality requested reconsideration; measures taken to avoid further excess PS.
observations.reason: LGU requested reconsideration; matter not fully resolved.

observations.recommendation: Require officials and employees to refund the total disallowed amount and refrain from incurring additional PEI contrary to law.
observations.managementAction: Some collections made; matter ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Review and remit unutilized or unexpended trust fund balances to the National Treasury for reversion as required.
observations.managementAction: From the unremitted balances, the remaining balance was reduced and steps taken to identify recipients and source agencies.
observations.reason: Some balances reduced; recipients/source agencies to be determined; some amounts unremitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and local finance committee evaluate the cost/benefit of credit financing/loan and in maintaining time deposits, in line with Section 4(8) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was asking for reconsideration; it was their belief that PS limitation had not been violated.
observations.reason: Management requested reconsideration; matter not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer Office to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake disposal thereof to prevent further deterioration of said properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer/GSO to conduct complete physical count of Property and
submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) in the prescribed format. Also, submit the RPCPPE to the Audit
Team not later than January 31 of each year;
b. Municipal Treasurer and OIC Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference
noted between the RPCPPE and accounting records in order not to
compromise the reliability of submitted reports;
c. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPE with lump
sum balances and at the same time secure the necessary documents to
reconcile them with the Inventory Report of GSO;
d. Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare an Inventory and Inspection of Report of
Unserviceable Property and all the necessary documents/reports for the
proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules
and regulations on disposal procedures; and
e. Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to abide
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 regarding the accounting and
reporting requirement of the Road Networks.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their
records to present reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial
statements;
b. Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their
dues on time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to
determine the accuracy of the receivables and its collectability; and
c. Municipal Treasurer submit copy of the document providing the legal basis
for the .5% assessment rate in the computation of levied special education
tax of Solar Philippines Calatagan Corporation at Barangay Biga and
Paraiso.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee
(LFC) to:
a. Undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget
on the basis of realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year and
conduct proper review of past collection performances.
b. Conduct periodic assessment to ascertain whether collection targets are
attained. The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analysed
and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses
in projections in the future.
We also recommended that regular reconciliation be made by the Municipal
Treasurer and OIC Municipal Accountant to avoid unreconciled balances. And if
the cause of discrepancy is justifiable, disclosure in the Notes to Financial
Statements is needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
determine the nature and amount of acquisition of these lots, as basis of
valuation;
b. Municipal Assessor to conduct sites inspection and prepare the reports on the
actual usage of each parcels of land and existence of unlawful occupants, if
any, and provide the said report to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation
purposes;
c. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the Municipality’s books of accounts the
unrecorded parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest
applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality; and
d. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor include in the
appropriations the titling of land owned by the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to a) prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accounting Office the List of Unreleased Checks in time for the closing of
the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable
accounts of the amount of unreleased checks in order to fairly present the
cash in bank and other affected accounts in the financial statements and b)
for the checks which have become stale, send written notice to the payees
for the existence of checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of
new checks; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of
reconciling items determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements on a timely basis for an accurate and reliable
Cash in Bank account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
determine the nature and amount of acquisition of these lots, as basis of
valuation;
b. Municipal Assessor to conduct sites inspection and prepare the reports on the
actual usage of each parcels of land and existence of unlawful occupants, if
any, and provide the said report to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation
purposes;
c. Municipal Accountant to recognize in the Municipality’s books of accounts the
unrecorded parcels of land using the market value indicated in the earliest
applicable Tax Declarations under the name of the Municipality; and
d. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Assessor include in the
appropriations the titling of land owned by the Municipal Government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Bids and Award Committee to strictly observe the provisions of the RIRR of
RA 9184 in the awarding of contract to legitimate/eligible contractor; and
b. Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents to consider that
these claims are allowable in audit.

We further recommended for a written justification from the BAC Chairman for the noted
lapses and inconsistencies in the procurement process such as: a) non-conduct of pre
procurement conference; b) non posting of the Notice of Award/Notice of Proceed
together with approved contract; c) failure to observe pertinent provision of 2016
Revised IRR; and presence of equipment in the contract for the construction of sanitary
landfill.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC Municipal Accountant
and other concerned municipal employees to submit immediately the lacking
documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to consider transaction
allowable in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development
Council and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to determine the
viability of the projects to be funded out of 20 percent Development Fund before
the preparation of the Annual Investment Plan to ensure its full implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office
(MDRRM) Council to prepare a Local Risk Reduction Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which identify and specify the PPAs to be
implemented;
b. Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office
(MDRRM) to enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the
Municipality under 70% Mitigation Fund, Continuing Appropriation and Trust
Liability – MDRRMF. Have those programs completed within the intended
time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the constituents; and
c. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the utilization of 5% LDRRMF.
Ensure that program and projects are aligned with their intended
appropriation.

We recommended the Municipality to maximize the utilization of GAD Funds and
see to it that GAD related Programs/Projects/Activities will fully addressed and
adhere to the basic purpose and objectives for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
remit the real property tax share to all the barangays to augment the limited
financial resources and to allow the immediate use of the fund for regular
expenses and priority projects and in compliance with Article 362 (b) of the IRR of
the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Engineer to
submit the lacking documents as enumerated above by the Audit Team and COA-
TAS for the final inspection of the said projects and technical evaluation of the
contracts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer coordinate with the contractor for
the refund of the cost deficiency noted by the COA TAS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal Accountant
to observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial
statements in accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No.
2016-004 to assists users in understanding the financial statements and to
provide them with information relevant to decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the
GPPB in printed and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance
with the provision of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC Municipal Accountant
to:
a. reconcile employees’ records at GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the
affected employees on the unremitted balances and immediately remit the
amounts due to these agencies and identify the cause/s of the over remittance
for some accounts and make the proper adjustments to avoid the incurrence of
unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties; and
b. review the taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors and
ensure that these taxes are remitted on the prescribed period to avoid
penalties and surcharges.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following: • The Municipal Treasurer/GSO to conduct complete physical count of Property and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the prescribed format. Also, submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year; • Municipal Treasurer and OIC Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference noted between the RPCPPE and accounting records in order not to compromise the reliability of submitted reports; • Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPE with lump sum balances and at the same time secure the necessary documents to reconcile them with the Inventory Report of GSO; • Municipal Treasurer/GSO to prepare an Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable Property and all the necessary documents/reports for the proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules and regulations on disposal procedures; and • Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to abide with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 regarding the accounting and reporting requirement of the Road Networks.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the following:
 The Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer prepare inventory list
by category and perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE in order to
determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of
the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies,
whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive
at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets,
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, prepare and
maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the reconciliation
of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
 The Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances. Secure the necessary documents to support
those PPEs to be able to reconcile with the Inventory Report of the GSO. Also, trace
those PPEs without depreciation and with negative balances to establish their correct
carrying value. Likewise, review the composition of the accounts under those PPEs
and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit,
including those PPEs disbursed in the Trust Fund. Secure proper documentation
before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous recording or for
inclusion to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and
regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as the designated GSO
maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to
present reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements;
 Municipal Assessor to Submit Monthly Report of Assessment of Real Properties
and Individual Tax Payers Assessment to the Municipal Treasurer for
reconciliation of their individual records;
 Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent
real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on time. Conduct
verification on those outstanding balances to determine the accuracy of the
receivables and its collectability; and
 Municipal Treasurer submit copy of the document providing the legal basis for
the .5% assessment rate of the real property of Solar Philippines Calatagan
Corporation in Barangay Biga and Paraiso that was used in the computation of
levied special education tax.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant and her staff to:
 Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on
receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of
the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts;
 Coordinate with the concerned officials and departments heads to obtain
information on those long outstanding accounts. Secure the necessary
documents to properly identify the creditor/local government unit to whom it’s
due. Once the specific local government unit had been identified, verify the
reported balances in the subsidiary ledgers through confirmation letters and
exhaust efforts to fully ascertain the validity and legality of those obligations; and
 Review the composition of the accounts under the Due to LGUs and identify the
appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper
documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording/inclusion in the Due to LGUs account to ensure that the amounts are
valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review in detail all entries made on the
Subsidy from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Special
Education Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s.
Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the
correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale
checks and the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipality’s
books of accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 The Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the accounts under the Due
to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the
audit. Secure proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting
entries for erroneous recording/inclusion in the Due to NGAS account to ensure
that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover
such adjustments.
 The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports if not yet
submitted and the check to the source agency for refund of the unexpended
balance. See to it that future fund transfers are fully implemented within the time
frame to avoid delay in liquidation. As to the balance of fund transfers of completed
projects with counterpart from the Municipality, identify the amounts to be remitted
to the source agency and to be reverted in the General Fund for appropriation.
 The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with the
concerned department heads to enforce the utilization and implementation of those
projects that were not yet implemented while pursue the continuation of those
ongoing utilization. Likewise, see to it that their respective time frames and
schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in its
implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to
strictly observe the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended for a written justification from the BAC Chairman for the noted
deficiencies and inconsistencies in the procurement process as a) non-conduct of pre-
procurement conference, b) non-submission of the NFCC c) non-submission of
contracts for review and evaluation by the Commission on Audit d) failure to provide
proof of invitation to bid as observers to a private group in a sector or discipline e)
posting of bid and performance securities not in accordance with the provisions f)
non-submission of bid evaluation and post-qualification reports and award of
contract to bidders in spite deficiencies in the bidding process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials
ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended
to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes. Any delay in the completion of
development projects means to slow down community development or realization of
local development objectives. We further recommended that the development
projects/activities shall be shown in itemized appropriations in the municipal budget and
any changes shall be made pursuant to Sections 321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160
and Articles 417 and 454(b) of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations or
through supplemental budgeting with only one exception which is the privilege
granted to Local Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan with respect to
augmentation of their offices’ budget through ordinance.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Sections 43 and 36 (c) of
Republic Act No. 9710 or “The Magna Carta of Women” that the LGU shall allocate at
least five (5) percent of its total appropriation for gender and development and shall
establish and maintain an operational GAD database which is one of the institutional
mechanisms for gender mainstreaming. We further recommended stringent
compliance with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated
January 12, 2016 of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and
Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic
and Development Authority to issue a policy directive during preparation and
implementation of GAD plan and budget; to plan, budget and prepare
accomplishment report as prescribed and to utilize the GAD budget based on the
DILG endorsed GAD plan and budget.

• Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
to enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the Municipality
under 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs completed within the
intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the
constituents;
• Require the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the utilization of 5% LDRRMF.
Ensure that program and projects are aligned with their intended appropriation;
and
• Require LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-
002. Also, coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the report in accordance with the prescribed format.

For the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended the following:
a. That the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Local School Board, adhere
strictly with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the prioritization and utilization
of Special Education Fund particularly for the operation and maintenance of public
schools, construction and repair of school buildings, facilities and equipment,
educational research, purchase of books and periodicals and sports development as
provided in Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2017 in order to ensure an
effective educational development within the locality;
b. That the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Budget Officer to assist the members of the LSB in the preparation of SEF budget
and in the identification and selection of programs, projects and activities in strict
compliance with the prescribed law and regulations of Department of Education
(DepEd), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of the
Interior and Local Government (DILG) and accounting and auditing rules of the
Commission on Audit; and
c. That the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Department of Education
officials to immediately submit the above mentioned supporting documents to
substantiate the transaction and the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that the
supporting documents were complete before processing of claims pursuant to
Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit.

• That the local government unit strictly adhere with the limitations on the grant of
monetization of leave credits pursuant to the provision of Section 23 of the
Omnibus Rules on Leave (Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of
Executive Order No. 292);
• Charge the monetization of the leave credits of the officials and employees of the
LGU in accordance with its appropriations ordinance and Section 3.2.2 of the
Department of Budget and Management Budget Circular No. 2002-1 dated January
14, 2002; and
• That Mr. Anthony R. Sayo, refund the corresponding amount for the excess
monetized leave credits totaling 45.25 days.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert
further efforts to communicate with GSIS and PAG-IBIG to confirm the balances
and/or to check with the records whether all premiums/loan repayments/contribution
deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all
employees were remitted in order to identify the affected employees on the
unremitted balance, if any. As to Due to BIR, exhaust all available means to secure
documents and journals to trace back those old balances and identify the specific tax
payers. Otherwise, if the mentioned agencies were able to confirm that they have no
outstanding claims from the Municipality, request for the reversion of such payables
following the applicable guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of the Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 of Commission on Audit, Department of Budget and Management,
Department of the Interior and Local Government, Governance Commission for GOCCs
and Department of National Defense dated January 8, 2015 prescribing the guidelines
for the documentary requirements of the granting of cash advances for confidential
expenses; submit its disbursement vouchers to the concerned COA Audit Team Leader
within seven (7) days after the release of the check covered with transmittal letter
enumerating the supporting documents in a sealed envelope and identify the expense
correctly in the municipal documents as confidential expenses instead of as intelligence
expenses.

We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the provisions of Republic Act
No. 9003 or “Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” that
espouses on the implementation of the 3R or the reduce, reuse and recycle concept
with focus on reduction of pollution and waste generation.

We recommended that the LGU organize the Municipal Council for the Protection of
Children (MCPC) and activate, strengthen, and sustain it pursuant to Section 15 of
Republic Act No. 9344 or the "Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel
concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the
Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part III of CY 2018 AAR
and on every AAR thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections 321
and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 454(b)of the Act Implementing Rules
and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging careless
preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of
planning and budgeting at the local level.

We recommended that the Municipality consider carefully and reevaluate thoroughly
its credit financing policy in light of the indicators that the existing loans should have
not been resorted to or availed with to implement the local government
infrastructures or development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads concerned
particularly its local finance committee to evaluate the cost/benefit of credit
financing/loan and in maintaining time deposits, in line with Section 4(8) of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor task the Human Resource
Management Office to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on the actual
workloads and manpower requirements of the Municipality for each
department/office to provide an accurate information/data on the need or
necessity to augment existing personnel complement through job order workers
employment and ensure that local resources were expended judiciously.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the following:
 The Inventory Committee headed by the Municipal Treasurer prepare inventory list
by category and perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE in order
to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of
the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies,
whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive
at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets,
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, prepare and
maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the
reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
 The Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances. Secure the necessary documents to support
those PPEs to be able to reconcile with the Inventory Report of the GSO. Also,
trace those PPEs without depreciation and with negative balances to establish their
correct carrying value. Likewise, review the composition of the accounts under
those PPEs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in
the audit, including those PPEs disbursed in the Trust Fund. Secure proper
documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording or for inclusion to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the
rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer as the designated GSO
maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had commented that there is a need to create a separate general services
office to spearhead the conduct of a complete physical count of PPE and coordinate with the
accounting office the results of the undertaking for reconciliation of balances recorded in the
books. Likewise, the Municpality had noted the other audit recommendation for its
compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to
present reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements;
 Municipal Assessor to Submit Monthly Report of Assessment of Real Properties
and Individual Tax Payers Assessment to the Municipal Treasurer for
reconciliation of their individual records;
 Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent
real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on time. Conduct
verification on those outstanding balances to determine the accuracy of the
receivables and its collectability; and
 Municipal Treasurer submit copy of the document providing the legal basis for
the .5% assessment rate of the real property of Solar Philippines Calatagan
Corporation in Barangay Biga and Paraiso that was used in the computation of
levied special education tax.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Treasurer had disclosed that one of the reasons for the discrepancy is the
recording of collections by the Province of the real property and special education taxes
wherein the Municipal Accountant had deducted only the share of the Municipality in the
said receivables. Likewise, they ensure that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the
Municipal Accounting Office are conducting reconciliation of their balances this CY 2019 to
settle the said discrepancies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant and her staff to:
 Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on
receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of
the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts;
 Coordinate with the concerned officials and departments heads to obtain
information on those long outstanding accounts. Secure the necessary documents
to properly identify the creditor/local government unit to whom it’s due. Once the
specific local government unit had been identified, verify the reported balances in
the subsidiary ledgers through confirmation letters and exhaust efforts to fully
ascertain the validity and legality of those obligations; and
 Review the composition of the accounts under the Due to LGUs and identify the
appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper
documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording/inclusion in the Due to LGUs account to ensure that the amounts are
valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant had commented that they will visit the Provincial Accounting
Office to reconcile their recorded receivables and payables. As to the long outstanding
balances, they said that those payables have no available documents to trace their origin
and the specific claimant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review in detail all entries made on the
Subsidy from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Special
Education Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s. Draw the
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance
of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Management had admitted that there is a discrepancy between the balance of Subsidy
from and to Other Funds in which they will coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to
check the details of the discrepancy.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale
checks and the Municipal Accountant make proper adjustments in the municipality’s
books of accounts to fairly present its financial statements as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Management had commented that they will comply with the recommendation and see
to it that adjusting entries will be made to correct the affected accounts in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
 The Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the accounts under the Due
to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the
audit. Secure proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries
for erroneous recording/inclusion in the Due to NGAS account to ensure that the
amounts are valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such
adjustments.
 The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports if not yet
submitted and the check to the source agency for refund of the unexpended
balance. See to it that future fund transfers are fully implemented within the time
frame to avoid delay in liquidation. As to the balance of fund transfers of completed
projects with counterpart from the Municipality, identify the amounts to be remitted
to the source agency and to be reverted in the General Fund for appropriation.
 The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with the
concerned department heads to enforce the utilization and implementation of those
projects that were not yet implemented while pursue the continuation of those
ongoing utilization. Likewise, see to it that their respective time frames and
schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in its
implementation.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant had commented that they submit liquidation reports to the source
agency and issue checks for the return of the unexpended balances. However, those
balances reflected in the books represent amounts with local counterpart and thus
requested the source agency if they can utilize it for other projects. As to the identified items
in the Due to NGAs for reclassification, they inquired as to the appropriate account to be
used to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to
strictly observe the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended for a written justification from the BAC Chairman for the noted
deficiencies and inconsistencies in the procurement process as a) non-conduct of
pre-procurement conference, b) non-submission of the NFCC c) non-submission of
contracts for review and evaluation by the Commission on Audit d) failure to provide
proof of invitation to bid as observers to a private group in a sector or discipline e)
posting of bid and performance securities not in accordance with the provisions f)
non-submission of bid evaluation and post-qualification reports and award of
contract to bidders in spite deficiencies in the bidding process.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The management had noted the audit recommendation and see to it that the deficiencies
noted in the observations are strictly monitored in the succeeding procurement projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials
ensure effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is primarily intended
to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes. Any delay in the completion of
development projects means to slow down community development or realization of
local development objectives. We further recommended that the development
projects/activities shall be shown in itemized appropriations in the municipal budget and
any changes shall be made pursuant to Sections 321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160
and Articles 417 and 454(b) of the Act Implementing Rules and Regulations or through
supplemental budgeting with only one exception which is the privilege granted to Local
Chief Executive and the Sangguniang Bayan with respect to augmentation of their
offices’ budget through ordinance.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The head of the MPDC explained that the construction/rehabilitation of multi-purpose hall
remained unimplemented due to the conversion of the whole Barangay into industrial zone,
thus the said pursuit of the project would be impractical. They also added that the land
owned by the residents of the Barangay had already been purchased by a private group.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Sections 43 and 36 (c) of
Republic Act No. 9710 or “The Magna Carta of Women” that the LGU shall allocate at
least five (5) percent of its total appropriation for gender and development and shall
establish and maintain an operational GAD database which is one of the institutional
mechanisms for gender mainstreaming. We further recommended stringent
compliance with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated
January 12, 2016 of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and
Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic
and Development Authority to issue a policy directive during preparation and
implementation of GAD plan and budget; to plan, budget and prepare
accomplishment report as prescribed and to utilize the GAD budget based on the DILG
endorsed GAD plan and budget.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had commented that the GAD Plan and Budget was regularly submitted
to the Provincial Planning and Development Office of Batangas. The said plan was prepared
before March of every year. However, they also noted the recommendations for immediate
implementation.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the forgoing observations, we recommended the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
to enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the Municipality
under 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs completed within the
intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the

 Require the Municipal Budget Officer to monitor the utilization of 5% LDRRMF.

and
 Require LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-
002. Also, coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the report in accordance with the prescribed format.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The MDRRM Officer had noted the audit recommendations. Likewise, they commented that
they will submit justification for the deficiencies included in the audit findings.

c. That the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Department of Education
officials to immediately submit the above mentioned supporting documents to
substantiate the transaction and the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensure that the
supporting documents were complete before processing of claims pursuant to

observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had commented that the ₱2,008,000.00 was intended for the purchase of
lot for senior high school. As to the charging of expenses in the SEF, they commented that
the expenses relative to the implementation of alternative learning system was included in
160 the valid expenditures under the old guidelines but was unaware of the updated joint circular
mentioned in the audit findings.

observations.recommendation:
For the foregoing, we recommended the following:
 That the local government unit strictly adhere with the limitations on the grant of
monetization of leave credits pursuant to the provision of Section 23 of the
Omnibus Rules on Leave (Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of
Executive Order No. 292);
 Charge the monetization of the leave credits of the officials and employees of the
LGU in accordance with its appropriations ordinance and Section 3.2.2 of the
Department of Budget and Management Budget Circular No. 2002-1 dated January
14, 2002; and
 That Mr. Anthony R. Sayo, refund the corresponding amount for the excess
monetized leave credits totaling 45.25 days.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Human Resource Officer had commented that they did not require an employee to
submit justification letter on monetization of leave credit beyond 50 per cent. Instead, they
only attach certification of leave credits in the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert
further efforts to communicate with GSIS and PAG-IBIG to confirm the balances
and/or to check with the records whether all premiums/loan repayments/contribution
deducted from the payroll of employees and Government Share (GS) for all
