payers. Otherwise, if the mentioned agencies were able to confirm that they have no

following the applicable guidelines.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The Municipal accountant had commented that those old balances will be coordinated to
those agencies for reconciliation and update of records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of the Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 of Commission on Audit, Department of Budget and
Management, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Governance
Commission for GOCCs and Department of National Defense dated January 8, 2015
prescribing the guidelines for the documentary requirements of the granting of cash
advances for confidential expenses; submit its disbursement vouchers to the
concerned COA Audit Team Leader within seven (7) days after the release of the
check covered with transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents in a
sealed envelope and identify the expense correctly in the municipal documents as
confidential expenses instead of as intelligence expenses.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Accountant had commented that they will comply with the audit
recommendation and ensured to submit the disbursement vouchers of confidential
expenses within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality fully implement the provisions of Republic Act
No. 9003 or “Philippine Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” that
espouses on the implementation of the 3R or the reduce, reuse and recycle concept
with focus on reduction of pollution and waste generation.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had commented that the sanitary landfill was not yet completed and
operational by the time they closed the open dumpsite, thus they resorted to putting their
garbage on the intended lot for the sanitary landfill instead of entering into contract with
private entity.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU organize the Municipal Council for the Protection of
Children (MCPC) and activate, strengthen, and sustain it pursuant to Section 15 of
Republic Act No. 9344 or the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had commented that they had a municipal ordinance for the creation of
MCPC which will be submitted once the said document had been secured. Likewise, for CY
2019, the Municipality had already allotted budget for the strengthening of the MCPC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel
concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the
Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part III of CY 2018 AAR
and on every AAR thereafter.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment:
The management had ensured the completion of the AAPSI within the prescribed period for
submission to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections
321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 454(b)of the Act Implementing
Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging careless
preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of
planning and budgeting at the local level.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Budget Officer had commented that they were not aware of those provisions
with regard to the re-alignment of funds of the Municipality. Likewise, they also commented
that they do augmentation to projects with insufficient budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality consider carefully and reevaluate thoroughly
its credit financing policy in light of the indicators that the existing loans should have
not been resorted to or availed with to implement the local government
infrastructures or development projects.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Treasurer had admitted that during CY 2018, the collection of the Municipality
had increased as compared to CY 2017. However, part of the cash balance was the receipt
of the loan from the LBP on installment basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the department heads concerned
particularly its local finance committee to evaluate the cost/benefit of credit
financing/loan and in maintaining time deposits, in line with Section 4(8) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The Municipal Treasurer had commented that said high interest savings account was
intended as contingency in case emergency situations arise. Likewise, they also commented
the amount of payment for the loan was already fixed, thus additional payments cannot be
made. However, they will take into account the option for restructuring to use the idle funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor task the Human Resource
Management Office to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on the actual
workloads and manpower requirements of the Municipality for each
department/office to provide an accurate information/data on the need or
necessity to augment existing personnel complement through job order workers
employment and ensure that local resources were expended judiciously.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had noted the recommendations for monitoring and evaluation the hiring
of job order employees. Likewise, they also commented that they will be conducting analysis
of the manpower requirement per office to identify the number of personnel in comparison
with the duties and responsibilities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to
review the trust liability accounts and remit the unutilized balances to the National
Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Management’s Comment/s:
The management had commented that the Municipality was already locating the recipients
of the program.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that:
• The LGU create Office of the GSO, if possible, and also an Inventory
Committee to conduct complete inventory taking of all the Municipal
properties particularly on those not included in the last year, prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) and submit to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor
concerned not later than January 31 of every year;
• Make strategic actions/plan on how to conduct a complete physical
inventory of all Municipal properties, engage and adopt all the necessary
means to accomplish the task/plan accordingly;
• The General Services Officer or the Assistant Municipal Treasurer exert
more effort to reconcile Inventory Report and Property records with that
of the Accounting records. Require person responsible/accountable to
request for relief from accountability to the Commission on Audit for
those properties that are missing and could not be accounted for;
• The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer/Assistant
Municipal Treasurer maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards
and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both
records; and
• The Municipal Accountant record all the donated properties in the books
and the Municipal Budget appropriate in its annual budget the cost of
transfer and titling of the 18 parcels of land in the name of the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET
Receivables. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the accurate balances of the accounts as of the accounting period.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as well as the
Sanggunian Bayan Members, adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers.
Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax
delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to adhere strictly to the provision of the aforesaid PPSAS by classifying
the current portion of long-term loans payable that is due for settlement within a
year from end of reporting period as part of the current liabilities in the Statement
of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development
Council and all other concerned officials to: a) ensure full utilization of the 20%
Development Fund for the efficient and effective attainment of desired socio-
economic development and environmental management outcomes. b) Include and
prioritize the budget for the completion of Day Care Center and Water Systems in
CY 2018. c) Make certain that all projects implemented will be usable by the
respective end users. d) Furnish the Regional Offices of DILG and DBM copy of
approved Annual Investment Plan containing, among others, the projects to be
funded out of the 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officials:
1. Strictly abide by the terms and conditions stipulated in the
Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the
DENR-EMB;
2. Create position for Municipal Environment and Natural Resources
Officer (MENRO) who can effectively and efficiently implement the
Municipal Solid Waste Management Program;
3. Strictly implement and cause the immediate and effective
rehabilitation of the controlled dumpsite and eventually the
construction and maintenance of sanitary landfill in accordance
with RA 9003; and
4. Expedite and exert more efforts for the approval and implementation
of the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to be more proficient with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of the
Revised RA 9184. Submit justification on the delays incurred in the construction of
the Municipal Cemetery, and if warranted, collect liquidated damages. Clarify the
inconsistencies with the documents submitted and the Municipal Accountant to
submit all the documentary requirements on the procurement of infrastructure
projects in order to validate the propriety of the transactions. Likewise, we
recommended strict compliance with all the provisions of the implementing rules
and regulations of the Government Procurement Act.

We recommended that the Management strictly effect the necessary
processes to expedite the implementation of the not-yet started and on-going
projects for the timely and immediate benefits of the municipality’s constituents.
Strategize and organize immediately the composition of the Board of Directors of
Calatagan Water District and exert more effort for the Salintubig project to be
usable and operational.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Budget Officer to allocate fund for the MOOE of Municipal Disaster Risk and
Reduction Management Office. Revert to the LDRRMF the total amount of ₱104,874.68
and ₱244,562.24 for current and previous year non-related expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board
to utilize fully the Special Education Fund for the benefit of the students and
formulate a three-year SEF program considering the programs/projects
enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 as implemented by DedEd, DBM
and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017. Submit the
necessary documents to support the claim of ₱77,000.00 to avoid suspension in
audit.

We recommended that the Management stop the practice of deducting
personal loans contracted by the employees in their personal capacity and stop from
acting as collecting and remitting agent of unauthorized institution. Likewise,
strictly comply with the GAA provision that monthly take home pay of employee
shall not be lower than Four Thousand Pesos (₱4,000.00).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
municipal officers to revisit and upgrade the Municipality’s implementing
guidelines on Aid to Individual in Crisis Situation (AICS) program. Adopt a clear
cut policy to ensure that the standard procedures and correct assistance were
distributed equally for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the municipal records with the Provincial Accountant and
settle discrepancies between the balances of reciprocal accounts so that the
Municipality will be able to present the correct balances of inter-agency receivables
and payables in the financial statements.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial Accountant
the copies of the JEVs showing all the transactions recorded in the books of
accounts of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s books of
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of
the Sanggunian include among its top priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly those
for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation period of
ten (10) years as mandated by law.

observations.managementAction:
The management has accomplished the following:
• Inventory of equipment and properties on-going.
• 2015 and 2016 inventory of equipment completed.
• Determination of properties that are missing on-going.
• Documentation on-going.
• Budget is proposed for titling of donated properties.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables. Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the accurate balances of the accounts as of the accounting period. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as well as the Sanggunian Bayan Members, adopt measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers. Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant will coordinate with the Accounting Office of the Province for reconciliation on collections made by them and draw Journal Entry Voucher for the necessary entries to RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET Income.

observations.managementAction: For 2017, the donor vehicles’ temporary plates are requested; the Izusu dump truck transfer already transferred. The JEV will follow for donated motor vehicles. The Accounting Office is now strict in requiring complete documents for disbursement of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to: 1. Strictly implement the pertinent governing rules and regulations provided in the Revised Revenue Code 2014 in the enforcement of collections from delinquent tenants. 2. Effect the imposition of surcharges to defaulting lessees as provided in the lease contract. 3. Facilitate the execution and immediate issuance of new Lease Contract with all tenants and the Municipality. 4. Exert more efforts and strategies to improve the economic and financial condition of the Market. 5. Create ordinances to help promote the Market.

observations.reason: Lacking legal expert to assess situation; Management is looking for a legal expert to help with the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: 1. Bids and Awards Committee to be cautious in the performance of their functions. 2. Strictly adhere with the provisions promulgated in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. 3. Members of the BAC and the Technical Working Group to submit a written justification for the lapses cited above with supporting documents if applicable for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

The Municipal Engineer is instructed to keep himself abreast with the IRR. The Municipal Accountant will submit all the lacking documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 6 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officials: 1. Create position for Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) who can focus effectively and efficiently implement the Municipal Solid Waste Management Program; 2. Strictly implement and cause the immediate and effective rehabilitation of the controlled dumpsite and eventually the construction and maintenance of sanitary landfill in accordance with RA 9003; and 3. Expedite and exert more efforts for the approval and implementation of the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan and its implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan is set to be implemented. Bidding documents are already prepared. The Construction of Sanitary Landfill is targeted to be implemented in the 3rd Quarter of 2018. Loan application to finance the same was already approved by the Land Bank of the Philippines. If the rules on budgeting permit, creation of a position for MENRO will be given consideration.
observations.reason: Funds from EMB were just released in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the concerned to: a) closely monitor the implementation of BUB projects and CSO; b) make supplementary research and promotion to gain marketing sales; and c) help resolve conflicts among members of CSO to promote cooperation and growth of the CSO.

observations.reason: Timing difference in the receipt of IRA; lack of interest of some members of the CSOs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council to ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the efficient and effective attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes. observations.managementAction: Projects are now publicized. observations.reason: Timing difference in the receipt of IRA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563. Refund the total amount of ₱62,100.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) ensure that all GAD mechanisms and processes for mainstreaming are in place; b) create monitoring and evaluation team; c) ensure that GAD Plan and Budget address specific gender issues; d) exert extra efforts to establish the GAD database as basis for planning, budgeting and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: GAD database is partially completed. The management will exert efforts to institutionalize gender equality and women's empowerment and to complete GAD database. Some items are on-going.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
the concerned Offices, in the absence of General Services Officer to expedite the transfer of Izusu dump truck in the name of the municipality, speed up the process of securing red plates and ensure that motor vehicles will be marked and used for official business only.
b) the Municipal Treasurer Office to prepare Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake disposal thereof to prevent further deterioration of said properties.

Members of the BAC and the Technical Working Group to submit a written justification for the lapses cited above with supporting documents if applicable for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officials:
Create position for Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) who can focus effectively and efficiently implement the Municipal Solid Waste Management Program.
Strictly implement and cause the immediate and effective rehabilitation of  the  controlled  dumpsite and eventually the construction and  maintenance  of sanitary landfill  in accordance with RA 9003; and
Expedite and exert more efforts for the approval of the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan and its implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the Officials concerned to: a) closely monitor the implementation of BUB projects and improve further the operation of each CSO, b) make supplementary research and promotion to gain marketing sales; and c) help resolve conflicts among members of CSO to promote cooperation and growth of the community.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council to ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the efficient and effective attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Engineer to publicize the projects/programs/activities in conspicuous places within the agency premises and in the project venue or notify the public on its essential features/elements in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2014.

The Municipal Budget Officer to allocate fund for the maintenance and operation (MOOE) of Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Office, charge against the General Fund. Revert to the LDRRMF the total amount of ₱244,562.24.
The MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council.

We amended our previous years’ recommendations that: The LGU create Office of the GSO, if possible,  and also an Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory taking of all the Municipal properties particularly on those not included in the last year, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year; The General Services Officer reconcile Inventory Report and Property records with that of the Accounting records. Require person responsible/accountable to request for relief from accountability to the Commission on Audit for those properties that are missing and could not be accounted for; The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records; and The Municipal Accountant record all the donated properties in the books and the Municipal Budget appropriate in its annual budget the cost of transfer and titling of the 18 parcels of land in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU employee was purposely designated to do the inventory of properties. CY 2016 additions to PPE were 100% recorded and accounted for. Reconciliation of PPE of prior years was on-going.
observations.reason:
Time constraintand lack of manpower hindered the LGU to complete the physical count of all properties. Office of the GSO has not yet been created due to budget limitation.
Request for Relief of Accountability has not yet been done due to on-going inventory and reconciliation.
The LGU only maintains soft copies of lapsing schedule, preparation of PPELC and Property Ledger Cards still on going.
The budget for titling is still pending but request for funding already made. The Management, if possible, will allot budget for titling of donated lots which are publicly used as road lots while school donated lots will be transferred to DepEd once the lots were titled already.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Bids and Awards Committee evaluate properly  the eligibility  documents submitted by the  bidders. Automatically disqualify the bidder who failed to submit the required financial and technical documents. Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group submit the lacking documents to the Auditor concerned for further evaluation and  ensure completeness of bidding documents to establish propriety of bidding process; invite three (3) observers to sit in its bidding proceedings; post the Notice of Award at PhilGEPS; adopt the Standard Forms and Standard Bidding Documents for the procurement of infrastructure projects or civil works prescribed by the Government Procurement Policy Board; and Municipal Accountant-OIC refrain from processing claims without complete documentation.

The LGU failed to include some pertinent documents like Letter to Observers, Posting of Notice of Award and others due to volume of transactions processed by the Accounting Dept.
